verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2, also called hK2) is a serine protease secreted by prostate epithelial cells. It is synthesized as a preproprotein, processed through the secretory pathway, and released as a soluble enzyme into seminal plasma and prostate secretions. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI-anchor signal, and no documented stable outer-leaflet membrane anchor. Checking all 5 contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer ecto-form on outer leaflet; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no outer-leaflet display even in prostate cells; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM minority pool or vesicular cycling to PM outer face; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant covalent or non-covalent tethering to a TM partner on the outer leaflet documented. Clinical programs (e.g., PSMA-targeting strategies) do not target KLK2 on the cell surface. KLK2 is secreted-only.
